# function: jsonencode

Returns a JSON string for the given value. Omit the second argument to match Terraform's compact `jsonencode` output, or pass an indentation string such as two spaces or a tab to produce formatted JSON.

## Example Usage

```hcl
locals {
  data = {
    foo = "bar"
    fiz = {
      fizzy = "buzzy"
    }
  }

  indent        = "  "
  indented_json = provider::risqaws::jsonencode(local.data, local.indent)
}
```

This produces:

```json
{
  "foo": "bar",
  "fiz": {
    "fizzy": "buzzy"
  }
}
```


## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
jsonencode(value dynamic, indent string...) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `value` (Dynamic, Nullable) 
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `indent` (Variadic, String)
